title: Update repository docs, skills, and checked-in templates
description: Rewrite repository-facing docs and skill/template files so public instructions, titles, command examples, and path references match `agent-takt`, `agent_takt`, `takt`, and `.takt` consistently.
acceptance_criteria: `README.md` and `CLAUDE.md` use the new project/CLI/module/runtime names required by the spec.
The listed files in `docs/` use `takt` commands and `src/agent_takt/` or `.takt/` paths where appropriate.

Checked-in `.agents/skills/**/*.md`, `.claude/skills/**/*.md`, and `templates/agents/*.md` no longer contain outdated `uv run orchestrator` or `.orchestrator/` references where the spec requires replacement.
No repository-facing doc update changes bead JSON schemas, workflow semantics, or unrelated narrative content beyond the rename.

handoff_summary.completed: Updated README.md, CLAUDE.md, docs/development.md, docs/onboarding.md, docs/tui.md, docs/multi-backend-agents.md, docs/scheduler-telemetry.md, and skills/spec-management/SKILL.md. All uv run orchestrator → uv run takt, src/codex_orchestrator/ → src/agent_takt/, .orchestrator/ → .takt/, ORCHESTRATOR_RUNNER → AGENT_TAKT_RUNNER (with legacy fallback notes preserved), package name codex-agent-orchestration → agent-takt, and CLI command orchestrator → takt. Verified .agents/skills/**/*.md, .claude/skills/**/*.md, and templates/agents/*.md already had no outdated references.
handoff_summary.remaining: docs/memory/conventions.md line 27 still references .orchestrator/worktrees/ but is outside this bead's scope (not in expected_files or expected_globs).
handoff_summary.risks: None. No runtime behavior was changed — only documentation and command examples were updated.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: None. These are documentation-only changes with no overlap with source code or test files.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: ORCHESTRATOR_RUNNER is retained in docs as a documented legacy fallback per the spec requirement. docs/memory/conventions.md was left untouched as it is out of scope for this bead.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: No test changes. Documentation beads do not run the test suite.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: docs/memory/conventions.md line 27 still contains a .orchestrator/worktrees/ reference. This file is outside the bead's declared scope and should be addressed separately if needed.

conflict_risks: None. These are documentation-only changes with no overlap with source code or test files.

execution_history.summary: Updated all repository-facing docs and skill files to use agent-takt, takt CLI, src/agent_takt/, and .takt/ paths consistently.

guardrails.template_text:
# Documentation Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Update documentation and examples that explain the assigned behavior without changing runtime feature behavior.

Allowed actions:
- Edit docs, examples, and explanatory text tied to the assigned bead.
- Focus only on files listed in the bead's touched_files and changed_files fields. Do not read or modify unrelated documentation.
- Align documentation with existing code and validated behavior.
- Identify when implementation or tests must land first and block with a handoff.

Disallowed actions:
- Change runtime behavior, production code paths, or feature logic.
- Invent undocumented behavior that is not present in the codebase.
- Approve code quality or test completeness as a substitute for review.
- Do not run the test suite. Testing is the tester agent's responsibility.

Efficiency constraints:
- Do not read the full codebase for context. Focus on the changed files and their immediate surroundings.
- Keep documentation updates concise and proportional to the change.

Expected outputs:
- Return JSON with `outcome` set to `completed` (documentation beads always complete; use `verdict` for pass/fail) and `summary` as a one-line description of what was documented.
- Return JSON with structured verdict fields for every run: `verdict`, `findings_count`, and `requires_followup`.
- Use `verdict=approved`, `findings_count=0`, and `requires_followup=false` when documentation is complete with no gaps.
- Use `verdict=needs_changes`, set `findings_count` to the number of unresolved documentation gaps, set `requires_followup=true`, and include `block_reason` when implementation must land first or documentation cannot be completed.
- Keep `completed`, `remaining`, and `risks` as concise narrative context only; they do not override the structured verdict fields.
- Accurate updated docs and clear next-agent recommendations when code changes are required first.
